annotation.text:
This Printing as a Service example stream demonstrates a simplified version of a DigitalRoute customer use case.
For this example, the customer is 'AlphaPrint'. They sell printing as a service and charge their customers based on actual usage.

In this example stream, you will learn how to collect different usage events, for example, the number of pages printed, the ink level, and the health status of the printers.

After the data is collected in the stream, you can see how it is enriched and aggregated by different functions before it is sent to its final destination, in this case, the Log function.

This example stream is explained in detail here: https://infozone.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/DAZ/pages/7847658/Printing+As+A+Service.
The Data aggregator sums up the monthly amount of printed paper sheets per printer.
Simulates enriching the usage data summaries with the customer and product master data from an external system.

Renames the input data to match the format required by the downstream system. Then, stores the data in a log. 

This step substitutes data being sent to a billing system.

This step checks the daily ink level per customer and triggers an ink order if needed.

The Route function checks the ink level. If the "low ink" threshold is reached, the Deduplicate function filters out any duplicated "low ink alert" records, ensuring that only one 'low ink' event is processed daily.
Simulates enriching the usage data summaries with the customer and product master data from an external system.

Renames the input data to match the format required by the downstream system. Then stores the data in a log. 

This step substitutes data being sent to an ink replenishment ordering system.

This part checks the daily health status of printers and sends a request for maintenance if needed.

The Route function checks the printer health status (normal or maintenance required). Then the Deduplicate function filters out duplicated records to ensure the 'health status check' event is only processed once per printer per day.
Simulates enriching the usage data summaries with the customer and product master data from an external system.

Renames the input data as desired. Then stores the data in a log. 

This step substitutes for sending a maintenance service order.
Generates sample records with printer data (printed pages, ink level, printer health status, etc.). This step substitutes real data input.

Filters out erroneous and duplicate records.
All records that are filtered by the Validate function can be further processed and corrected with the help of Data Correction.
